questions.testName: June 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt:
$f(x) = 8x + 4$
The function $f$ gives the estimated height, in feet, of a willow tree $x$ years after its height was first measured. Which statement is the best interpretation of 4 in this context?
questions.questionText:
$f(x) = 8x + 4$
The function $f$ gives the estimated height, in feet, of a willow tree $x$ years after its height was first measured. Which statement is the best interpretation of 4 in this context?

choices.text: The tree will be measured each year for 4 years.
The tree is estimated to grow to a maximum height of 4 feet.
The estimated height of the tree increased by 4 feet each year.
The estimated height of the tree was 4 feet when it was first measured.

questions.testName: June 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: In the $xy$-plane, line $k$ has a slope of $-6$ and a $y$-intercept of $(0,48)$. What is the $x$-coordinate of the $x$-intercept of line $k$?
questions.questionText: In the $xy$-plane, line $k$ has a slope of $-6$ and a $y$-intercept of $(0,48)$. What is the $x$-coordinate of the $x$-intercept of line $k$?

questions.testName: June 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt:
The table gives the perimeters of similar triangles $TUV$ and $XYZ$, where $\overline{TU}$ corresponds to $\overline{XY}$. The length of $\overline{TU}$ is 9.
What is the length of $\overline{XY}$?
questions.questionText:

questions.testName: June 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: $A$ circle in the $xy$-plane has the equation $(x - 14)^2 + (y - k)^2 = 81$. Which of the following gives the center of the circle and its radius?
questions.questionText: $A$ circle in the $xy$-plane has the equation $(x - 14)^2 + (y - k)^2 = 81$. Which of the following gives the center of the circle and its radius?

choices.text: The center is at $(14, k)$ and the radius is 9.
The center is at $(k, 14)$ and the radius is 9.
The center is at $(14, k)$ and the radius is 81.
The center is at $(k, 14)$ and the radius is 81.

questions.testName: June 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: The town of Fillmore is planning its annual fireworks show. The equation $y = -16 (x - 6.9)^2 + 762$ gives the estimated height $y$, in feet, of a type of firework $x$ seconds after it is launched into the air. Which of the following is the best interpretation of the vertex of the graph of the equation in the $xy$-plane?
questions.questionText: The town of Fillmore is planning its annual fireworks show. The equation $y = -16 (x - 6.9)^2 + 762$ gives the estimated height $y$, in feet, of a type of firework $x$ seconds after it is launched into the air. Which of the following is the best interpretation of the vertex of the graph of the equation in the $xy$-plane?

choices.text: This type of firework reaches an estimated maximum height of 6.9 feet 762 seconds after it is launched into the air.
This type of firework reaches an estimated maximum height of 16 feet 762 seconds after it is launched into the air.
This type of firework reaches an estimated maximum height of 762 feet 6.9 seconds after it is launched into the air.
This type of firework reaches an estimated maximum height of 762 feet 16 seconds after it is launched into the air.

questions.testName: June 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: The graph of $y = f(x)$ is shown where $f(x) = \alpha x^3 + bx^2 + cx + d$ and $\alpha, b, c,$ and $d$ are constants. For how many distinct values of $x$ does $f(x) = 0$?
questions.questionText: The graph of $y = f(x)$ is shown where $f(x) = \alpha x^3 + bx^2 + cx + d$ and $\alpha, b, c,$ and $d$ are constants. For how many distinct values of $x$ does $f(x) = 0$?

questions.testName: June 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: Point O is the center of a unit circle in the $xy$-plane, and its coordinates are $(0,0)$. Point A lies on the unit circle, and its coordinates are $(0,1)$. Point P on the unit circle is obtained by rotating point A $\frac{7\pi}{6}$ radians about the origin in the counterclockwise direction. What are the coordinates of point P?
questions.questionText: Point O is the center of a unit circle in the $xy$-plane, and its coordinates are $(0,0)$. Point A lies on the unit circle, and its coordinates are $(0,1)$. Point P on the unit circle is obtained by rotating point A $\frac{7\pi}{6}$ radians about the origin in the counterclockwise direction. What are the coordinates of point P?

questions.questionText: In the figure, $AC = CD$. The measure of angle $EBC$ is 42°, and the measure of angle $ACD$ is 108°. What is the value of $x$?

questions.testName: June 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: The positive number $a$ is 3,900% of the number $c$, and $c$ is 25% of the number $b$. If $a - b = wc$, where $w$ is a constant, what is the value of $w$?
questions.questionText: The positive number $a$ is 3,900% of the number $c$, and $c$ is 25% of the number $b$. If $a - b = wc$, where $w$ is a constant, what is the value of $w$?

questions.testName: June 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: Line $j$ is defined by $3x + 4y = 28$. Line $k$ is parallel to line $j$ in the $xy$-plane. An equation of line $k$ is $24x + ry = 24$, where $r$ is a constant. If line $k$ passes through the point $(0, b)$, what is the value of $b$?
questions.questionText: Line $j$ is defined by $3x + 4y = 28$. Line $k$ is parallel to line $j$ in the $xy$-plane. An equation of line $k$ is $24x + ry = 24$, where $r$ is a constant. If line $k$ passes through the point $(0, b)$, what is the value of $b$?

questions.testName: June 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: The dot plot shows the distribution of capacity for a set of capacitors, set A, which a researcher used for a certain experiment. For another experiment, the researcher used a different set of capacitors, set B. Set B has the same number of capacitors as set A but the capacity of each capacitor of set B is 13 microfarads (μF) greater than the capacity of each respective capacitor in set A. Which of the following is true about the capacities of the capacitors in set B?
questions.questionText: The dot plot shows the distribution of capacity for a set of capacitors, set A, which a researcher used for a certain experiment. For another experiment, the researcher used a different set of capacitors, set B. Set B has the same number of capacitors as set A but the capacity of each capacitor of set B is 13 microfarads (μF) greater than the capacity of each respective capacitor in set A. Which of the following is true about the capacities of the capacitors in set B?

choices.text: The mean capacity is 8 μF, and the range of capacities is 12 μF.
The mean capacity is 8 μF, and the range of capacities is 25 μF.
The mean capacity is 21 μF, and the range of capacities is 12 μF.
The mean capacity is 21 μF, and the range of capacities is 25 μF.

questions.testName: August 2024 International Form A SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: A scientist studying the life cycle of butterflies counted the number of butterflies in a certain habitat each day for 46 days. On June 15, there were 168 butterflies in the habitat. The percent increase in the number of butterflies in the habitat from May 1 to June 15 was 31.25%. How many butterflies were in the habitat on May 1?

questions.prompt:
$f(x)=k(1.83)^{x}$
The function $f$ is defined by the given equation, where $k$ is a constant. The value of $f(x)$ increases by $p$% for every increase of $x$ by 1. For which of the following functions, where $k$ is a constant, does the value of $g(x)$ increase by $p$% for every increase of $x$ by $\frac{1}{4}$?

questions.testName: August 2024 International Form A SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: A line intersects two parallel lines, forming four acute angles and four obtuse angles. The measure of one of the acute angles is $(9x - 490)°$. The sum of the measures of one of the acute angles and three of the obtuse angles is $(-18x + w)°$. What is the value of $w$?

questions.prompt: The exponential function $f$ is defined by $f(x)= ab^x$, where $a$ and $b$ are positive constants. If $f(n-1)=f(n)+\frac{82}{100}f(n-1)$, where $n$ is a constant, what is the value of $b$?

questions.testName: August 2024 International Form A SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt:
$\frac{1}{R} = \frac{1}{p} + \frac{1}{q} + \frac{1}{s} + \frac{1}{13}$

A parallel electric circuit contains four resistors with resistances of $p$ ohms, $q$ ohms, $s$ ohms, and 13 ohms. The given equation relates the resistance $R$, in ohms, of the circuit, to the resistances of the four individual resistors it contains. Which equation correctly expresses $R$ in terms of $p$, $q$, and $s$?

questions.questionText: $A$ circle in the $xy$-plane has the equation $(x - 14)^2 + (y - k)^2 = 81$. Which of the following gives the center of the circle and its radius?

choices.text: The center is at $(14, k)$ and the radius is 9.
The center is at $(k, 14)$ and the radius is 9.
The center is at $(14, k)$ and the radius is 81.
The center is at $(k, 14)$ and the radius is 81.

questions.testName: June 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: The town of Fillmore is planning its annual fireworks show. The equation $y = -16 (x - 6.9)^2 + 762$ gives the estimated height $y$, in feet, of a type of firework $x$ seconds after it is launched into the air. Which of the following is the best interpretation of the vertex of the graph of the equation in the $xy$-plane?
questions.questionText: The town of Fillmore is planning its annual fireworks show. The equation $y = -16 (x - 6.9)^2 + 762$ gives the estimated height $y$, in feet, of a type of firework $x$ seconds after it is launched into the air. Which of the following is the best interpretation of the vertex of the graph of the equation in the $xy$-plane?

choices.text: This type of firework reaches an estimated maximum height of 6.9 feet 762 seconds after it is launched into the air.
This type of firework reaches an estimated maximum height of 16 feet 762 seconds after it is launched into the air.
This type of firework reaches an estimated maximum height of 762 feet 6.9 seconds after it is launched into the air.
This type of firework reaches an estimated maximum height of 762 feet 16 seconds after it is launched into the air.

questions.testName: June 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: The graph of $y = f(x)$ is shown where $f(x) = \alpha x^3 + bx^2 + cx + d$ and $\alpha, b, c,$ and $d$ are constants. For how many distinct values of $x$ does $f(x) = 0$?
questions.questionText: The graph of $y = f(x)$ is shown where $f(x) = \alpha x^3 + bx^2 + cx + d$ and $\alpha, b, c,$ and $d$ are constants. For how many distinct values of $x$ does $f(x) = 0$?

questions.testName: June 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: The product of a positive number $x$ and the number that is 13 less than $x$ is equal to 378. What is the value of $x$?
questions.questionText: The product of a positive number $x$ and the number that is 13 less than $x$ is equal to 378. What is the value of $x$?

questions.testName: June 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: Point O is the center of a unit circle in the $xy$-plane, and its coordinates are $(0,0)$. Point A lies on the unit circle, and its coordinates are $(0,1)$. Point P on the unit circle is obtained by rotating point A $\frac{7\pi}{6}$ radians about the origin in the counterclockwise direction. What are the coordinates of point P?
questions.questionText: Point O is the center of a unit circle in the $xy$-plane, and its coordinates are $(0,0)$. Point A lies on the unit circle, and its coordinates are $(0,1)$. Point P on the unit circle is obtained by rotating point A $\frac{7\pi}{6}$ radians about the origin in the counterclockwise direction. What are the coordinates of point P?

questions.testName: June 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: In the figure, $AC = CD$. The measure of angle $EBC$ is 42°, and the measure of angle $ACD$ is 108°. What is the value of $x$?
questions.questionText: In the figure, $AC = CD$. The measure of angle $EBC$ is 42°, and the measure of angle $ACD$ is 108°. What is the value of $x$?

questions.testName: June 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: The positive number $a$ is 3,900% of the number $c$, and $c$ is 25% of the number $b$. If $a - b = wc$, where $w$ is a constant, what is the value of $w$?
questions.questionText: The positive number $a$ is 3,900% of the number $c$, and $c$ is 25% of the number $b$. If $a - b = wc$, where $w$ is a constant, what is the value of $w$?

questions.testName: June 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: Line $j$ is defined by $3x + 4y = 28$. Line $k$ is parallel to line $j$ in the $xy$-plane. An equation of line $k$ is $24x + ry = 24$, where $r$ is a constant. If line $k$ passes through the point $(0, b)$, what is the value of $b$?
questions.questionText: Line $j$ is defined by $3x + 4y = 28$. Line $k$ is parallel to line $j$ in the $xy$-plane. An equation of line $k$ is $24x + ry = 24$, where $r$ is a constant. If line $k$ passes through the point $(0, b)$, what is the value of $b$?

questions.testName: June 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: The dot plot shows the distribution of capacity for a set of capacitors, set A, which a researcher used for a certain experiment. For another experiment, the researcher used a different set of capacitors, set B. Set B has the same number of capacitors as set A but the capacity of each capacitor of set B is 13 microfarads (μF) greater than the capacity of each respective capacitor in set A. Which of the following is true about the capacities of the capacitors in set B?
questions.questionText: The dot plot shows the distribution of capacity for a set of capacitors, set A, which a researcher used for a certain experiment. For another experiment, the researcher used a different set of capacitors, set B. Set B has the same number of capacitors as set A but the capacity of each capacitor of set B is 13 microfarads (μF) greater than the capacity of each respective capacitor in set A. Which of the following is true about the capacities of the capacitors in set B?

choices.text: The mean capacity is 8 μF, and the range of capacities is 12 μF.
The mean capacity is 8 μF, and the range of capacities is 25 μF.
The mean capacity is 21 μF, and the range of capacities is 12 μF.
The mean capacity is 21 μF, and the range of capacities is 25 μF.

questions.testName: May 2025 International Form B SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: A piece of string with a length of $108$ inches is cut into two parts. One part has a length of $x$ inches, and the other part has a length of $y$ inches. The value of $x$ is 8 more than 4 times the value of $y$. What is the value of $x$?
questions.questionText: A piece of string with a length of $108$ inches is cut into two parts. One part has a length of $x$ inches, and the other part has a length of $y$ inches. The value of $x$ is 8 more than 4 times the value of $y$. What is the value of $x$?

questions.testName: August 2024 International Form A SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: A scientist studying the life cycle of butterflies counted the number of butterflies in a certain habitat each day for 46 days. On June 15, there were 168 butterflies in the habitat. The percent increase in the number of butterflies in the habitat from May 1 to June 15 was 31.25%. How many butterflies were in the habitat on May 1?
questions.questionText: A scientist studying the life cycle of butterflies counted the number of butterflies in a certain habitat each day for 46 days. On June 15, there were 168 butterflies in the habitat. The percent increase in the number of butterflies in the habitat from May 1 to June 15 was 31.25%. How many butterflies were in the habitat on May 1?

questions.testName: August 2024 International Form A SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: For groups of 5 or more people, a tour guide charges \$9 for the first person in the group and \$6 for each additional person. Which function $g$ gives the total charge, in dollars, for a tour with a group of $n$ people, where $n \geq 5$?

questions.testName: August 2024 International Form A SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt:
$f(x)=k(1.83)^{x}$
The function $f$ is defined by the given equation, where $k$ is a constant. The value of $f(x)$ increases by $p$% for every increase of $x$ by 1. For which of the following functions, where $k$ is a constant, does the value of $g(x)$ increase by $p$% for every increase of $x$ by $\frac{1}{4}$?
questions.questionText:
$f(x)=k(1.83)^{x}$

questions.testName: August 2024 International Form A SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: A line intersects two parallel lines, forming four acute angles and four obtuse angles. The measure of one of the acute angles is $(9x - 490)°$. The sum of the measures of one of the acute angles and three of the obtuse angles is $(-18x + w)°$. What is the value of $w$?
questions.questionText: A line intersects two parallel lines, forming four acute angles and four obtuse angles. The measure of one of the acute angles is $(9x - 490)°$. The sum of the measures of one of the acute angles and three of the obtuse angles is $(-18x + w)°$. What is the value of $w$?

questions.testName: August 2024 International Form A SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: The exponential function $f$ is defined by $f(x)= ab^x$, where $a$ and $b$ are positive constants. If $f(n-1)=f(n)+\frac{82}{100}f(n-1)$, where $n$ is a constant, what is the value of $b$?
questions.questionText: The exponential function $f$ is defined by $f(x)= ab^x$, where $a$ and $b$ are positive constants. If $f(n-1)=f(n)+\frac{82}{100}f(n-1)$, where $n$ is a constant, what is the value of $b$?

questions.testName: August 2024 International Form A SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt:
$\frac{1}{R} = \frac{1}{p} + \frac{1}{q} + \frac{1}{s} + \frac{1}{13}$

A parallel electric circuit contains four resistors with resistances of $p$ ohms, $q$ ohms, $s$ ohms, and 13 ohms. The given equation relates the resistance $R$, in ohms, of the circuit, to the resistances of the four individual resistors it contains. Which equation correctly expresses $R$ in terms of $p$, $q$, and $s$?
questions.questionText:
$\frac{1}{R} = \frac{1}{p} + \frac{1}{q} + \frac{1}{s} + \frac{1}{13}$

A parallel electric circuit contains four resistors with resistances of $p$ ohms, $q$ ohms, $s$ ohms, and 13 ohms. The given equation relates the resistance $R$, in ohms, of the circuit, to the resistances of the four individual resistors it contains. Which equation correctly expresses $R$ in terms of $p$, $q$, and $s$?
